Industry Mar 31, 2026

SME Aviation: Your Aviation Construction Specialists

From concept to grand opening, SME Aviation leverages decades of experience to plan and successfully execute all types of aviation infrastructure projects.

Specialized aviation construction firms can differentiate by offering end-to-end project management from design through operational launch, reducing coordination complexity for airport and facility developers.

See Vertical Magazine →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Inside The Airbus A321XLR's Fuel Tank System

By adding the Rear Centre Tank, Airbus was able to increase the XLR's range to 4,700 nautical miles allowing it to fill the Boeing 757's shoes.

The Rear Centre Tank addition enables the A321XLR to compete directly in the long-range narrow-body segment previously dominated by the Boeing 757, addressing a critical market gap for carriers needing transatlantic capability without wide-body operating costs.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Quantum Helicopters: Back to basics

Quantum Helicopters joins Team M1 as training provider for U.S. Army Flight School Next bid.

Quantum Helicopters' partnership positions it to compete for a transformational Army pilot training contract that could reshape rotorcraft instruction methods.

See Vertical Magazine →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Flying Through San Francisco? New 6-Month Closure Could Impact Your Flight

The landing strip's six-month operational hiatus commenced yesterday.

A six-month runway closure at a major Bay Area airport will force traffic redistribution across regional facilities, creating scheduling challenges for airlines and potential capacity constraints during peak travel periods.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective risk control implementation requires organizations to document why residual risks are acceptable, creating accountability beyond mere hazard identification.

Industry Mar 31, 2026

How Middle East conflict is disrupting aviation parts and MRO flows

The ongoing conflict in the Middle East continues to affect millions of people, disrupting daily life and creating…...

Middle East tensions are fragmenting supply chains and maintenance schedules across global aviation networks, forcing operators to find alternative sourcing and routing strategies.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

NOAA Introduces Updated Icing and Turbulence Forecast

New system will provide higher-resolution weather data for aviation users.

Higher-resolution forecasting enables pilots and dispatchers to identify safer cruise altitudes and routing, potentially reducing fuel burns and flight time via more precise weather avoidance.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

End Of An Era: Emirates Cuts 62% Of Flights On Huge 615-Seat Airbus A380s

The world's highest-capacity double-decker layout will be disappearing, but before that, big cuts are planned...

Emirates' A380 fleet reduction signals the structural economics of ultra-high-capacity aircraft are unsustainable in post-pandemic demand patterns, likely accelerating retirement timelines across the industry.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

A strong safety culture directly reduces unreported hazards and near-misses, making voluntary reporting systems the foundation of effective Safety Management Systems.

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Palm Beach Airport for Trump

The name change could take effect as soon as July 1.

The rebranding of a major Florida airport signals how political figures are leveraging infrastructure naming rights to enhance their personal legacy and influence regional aviation identities.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 10 Easiest Airports To Transit Through In 2026

Most of these hubs are in Asia, balancing efficiency with amenities.

Asian hub airports are prioritizing streamlined passenger flows and modern facilities to compete for connecting traffic, signaling a regional shift in transit experience standards.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Press Release: Lufthansa Technik Malta completes first 787 cabin mod

Lufthansa Technik has successfully completed its first Boeing 787 Dreamliner cabin modification at its European Center of Excellence for widebody Base Maintenance Services in Malta. This strategic achievement marks an important step for Lufthansa Technik Malta’s growing widebody service portfolio....

Lufthansa Technik's Malta facility expanding into 787 cabin modifications signals European MRO providers are competing for high-value widebody retrofit work traditionally dominated by Asian carriers.

See Runway Girl Network →

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Mid-sized regional airports like CID serve as critical infrastructure nodes supporting both flight training pipelines and commercial air service in underserved markets.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Agentic AI: the future of space warfare

The future of space warfare will be determined by the speed of decision. As satellite constellations proliferate and adversaries field increasingly sophisticated counterspace capabilities, the ability to sense, analyze and act faster than an opponent will dictate who controls the space domain....

Space warfare dominance will hinge on autonomous AI decision-making speed rather than human reaction times as satellite constellations and counterspace threats accelerate tactical timelines.

See SpaceNews →

[Ukraine, Kyiv] – The Ukrainian defense-tech company General Cherry, one of Ukraine’s largest producers of...

Ukrainian defense manufacturers are accelerating partnerships with US companies to secure supply chains and technology transfer amid ongoing conflict, signaling broader defense-industrial realignment.

Red Cat Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: RCAT) (“Red Cat” or the “Company”), a U.S.-based provider of advanced...

Red Cat's acquisition of Apium strengthens its autonomous swarm technology capabilities, positioning it to compete in the rapidly expanding defense and commercial drone markets.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, requiring systemic solutions beyond individual awareness campaigns.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Up To 16 Hours: British Airways' 10 Longest Nonstop Flights From London [2026]

British Airways is the world's fifth-largest long-haul operator by number of flights. Only United Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Delta Air Lines have more long-haul services. Between now and the end of October, BA has an average of 95 daily long-haul departures from London, up from 91...

BA's expansion to 95 daily long-haul departures positions it to compete more aggressively in ultra-long-haul markets where operational efficiency on 16-hour routes directly impacts profitability.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Wingtra’s new white paper validates sub-3 cm accuracy across 102 flights

Wingtra, the leading manufacturer of drone aerial survey and mapping systems, today released a white...

Wingtra's validated sub-3cm accuracy across 102 flights establishes drone-based surveying as a cost-effective alternative to traditional manned aircraft for precision mapping missions.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

ITA Airways officially joins Star Alliance

Italian airline ITA Airways has marked its official accession to Star Alliance, which will become effective on April…...

ITA Airways' Star Alliance membership expands the alliance's European network and provides Italian carriers with enhanced transatlantic and global connectivity options.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airline, airport, and flight systems offers more immediate environmental gains than waiting for new aircraft technology adoption.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Antaris™ Raises $28 Million Series A to Accelerate AI-Driven Space Missions

Los Altos, California — Antaris™, creator of the AI-powered Antaris Intelligence™ platform that simplifies satellite design, simulation, manufacturing, and operations for ISR and communications satellite missions, today announced the first close […]...

AI-driven satellite design platforms are attracting significant venture capital, signaling investor confidence that automation can reduce development timelines and costs for intelligence and communications missions.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 31, 2026

More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East

The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. The U.S. Air Force A-10C Thunderbolt II jets have been actively supporting Operation Epic Fury in Iran...

Persistent A-10 deployments to the Middle East signal sustained U.S. commitment to close air support operations despite decades of potential retirement discussions.

See The Aviationist →

The name change hasn't been finalized yet, as it still requires FAA approval.

FAA approval remains the critical bottleneck for airport rebranding initiatives, creating potential delays even after local political consensus is reached.

Walsh's appointment signals IndiGo's strategic pivot toward enterprise-level operational maturity and global industry influence beyond its regional low-cost positioning.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 12 Airlines That Still Operate 4-Engine Passenger Jets

Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.

As quadjet operators dwindle, remaining airlines must optimize ultra-long-haul routes to justify increasingly expensive fuel and maintenance costs for these aircraft.

International Mar 31, 2026

Poland rules out Patriot redeployment after US request reported

The United States has informally asked Poland to consider redeploying one of its two Patriot air defense batteries…...

Poland's refusal to redeploy air defense systems signals broader European reluctance to weaken homeland protection amid ongoing regional security concerns.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches this year, according to a commercial launch executive, marking a sharp acceleration in the country’s launch cadence.

China's 140-launch target in 2026 represents a fundamental shift in global launch capacity dynamics, potentially reshaping satellite constellation deployment timelines and launch service pricing across the industry.

See SpaceNews →

International Mar 31, 2026

Iran war forces IATA to cut global seat capacity growth projections

The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has said that global capacity growth eased to 3.3% year on year…...

Geopolitical instability is causing IATA to downgrade growth forecasts, signaling airlines should reassess fleet expansion timelines and route planning.

Massive 25-Hour Flights: Qantas' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Qantas' ultra-long routes will require specialized crew scheduling and aircraft maintenance protocols to manage fatigue and operational complexity on these extended missions.

Military Mar 31, 2026

AI drones are now catching aerial threats like flying cops

XTEND is doubling down on one of the biggest challenges in modern warfare: how to stop hostile drones, without making things worse in the process. The AI-driven robotics company, backed by Eric Trump, has announced a new partnership with ParaZero Technologies as part of its broader $1.5 billion...

Military drone interception is shifting from destructive engagement to capture-and-neutralize tactics, reducing collateral damage risks in contested airspace.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Two EA-37Bs Arrive at RAF Mildenhall on Possible First Operational Deployment

Two EA-37B Compass Call aircraft stopped at RAF Mildenhall for fuel during what may be the type’s first operational deployment abroad, reports suggesting Turkey as their destination. Two U.S. Air Force EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft have arrived at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31,...

The EA-37B Compass Call's maiden operational deployment signals expanded U.S. electronic warfare capability projection into strategic regions, potentially addressing peer-competitor air defense systems.

See The Aviationist →

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

European aerospace competitiveness increasingly hinges on coordinated defense-oriented space investment rather than purely commercial ventures.
